summaries.2603.26164:
机器学习; 自然语言处理  
背景/任务是针对大语言模型训练中数据选择、混合和加权方法分散且难以集成的问题，提出一个统一框架。  
他们开发了DataFlex框架，集成多种动态数据优化方法，实验证明其能提升模型性能与训练效率。
summaries.2604.02029:
自然语言处理；机器学习
背景/任务是探讨语言模型中潜空间作为计算基质的兴起及其相较于显式标记空间的优势。
本文通过五个视角系统综述了潜空间的研究进展，并梳理了其机制、能力及未来方向。
summaries.2604.02329:
计算机视觉;生成模型
合成数据真实性不足限制了生成式逆渲染与正向渲染在真实场景的应用。
构建了大规模动态游戏数据集，并提出评估协议以提升逆渲染的跨数据集泛化与可控生成能力。
summaries.2604.02268:
机器学习;强化学习  
背景/任务：提升LLM智能体通过动态加载技能包执行任务的能力，减少推理时技能检索的噪声和开销，实现技能内化。  
方法：提出SKILL0框架，通过训练时从完整上下文逐步退出的课程学习方法，将技能内部化到模型参数中，实现零样本自主行为。
summaries.2604.02327:
计算机视觉；多模态学习  
背景/任务：视觉表示方法难以通过语言引导关注非显著物体，而多模态大模型的表示则偏向语言中心，影响通用视觉任务性能。  
作者提出可引导视觉表示，通过在视觉编码器中早期融合文本，使特征能够根据自然语言指令聚焦于任意目标，同时保持通用视觉任务的表现。
summaries.2604.01658:
人工智能;智能体与多智能体系统  
背景/任务：针对开放性问题，现有基于大语言模型的进化方法依赖固定启发式规则，限制了智能体自主性。  
工作内容：提出了自主多智能体进化框架CORAL，通过持久记忆、异步协作和心跳干预实现知识积累，在多项任务上取得突破性性能提升。
summaries.2604.02296:
计算机视觉;视频处理  
现有视频物体移除方法在处理物体移除后的物理交互（如碰撞）时效果不佳，导致结果不真实。  
论文提出VOID框架，通过生成对抗性数据集并利用视觉语言模型和视频扩散模型，实现物理合理的视频修复。
summaries.2604.01001:
计算机视觉;机器人学  
为克服现有模拟器缺乏3D空间一致性和动态场景更新能力的局限，提出了EgoSim这一闭环第一人称世界模拟器。  
它通过几何动作感知的观测模拟模型生成交互视频，并利用交互感知状态更新模块持续更新3D场景状态，同时构建了低成本数据采集管道以解决训练数据瓶颈。
summaries.2604.01202:
机器学习;自然语言处理  
研究背景是探索大语言模型在推理决策时是“先思考后决定”还是“先决定后思考”。  
研究发现模型在生成思维链前就已编码决策，通过线性探针可提前解码选择，且激活干预能翻转其决策和行为。
summaries.2604.02097:
计算机视觉;多模态学习  
背景/任务是研究如何统一跨模态理解与生成模型，以支持更高效的视觉推理和生成。  
他们提出了LatentUM模型，通过在共享潜在空间中表示所有模态，无需像素解码桥接，实现了跨模态推理和生成。
summaries.2604.01973:
计算机视觉；模式识别与机器学习  
现有视觉编码器在身份相关任务中，因身份与背景信息纠缠导致评估不可靠。  
作者提出NearID框架和数据集，通过对比学习增强身份表征，显著提升了身份判别准确率。
summaries.2604.01007:
人工智能工程；自主智能体
背景/任务：处理AI智能体在长期运行中的多模态记忆保留、组织和检索问题，其设计空间复杂难以手动探索。
做了什么：通过自主研究流程发现了Omni-SimpleMem框架，大幅提升了两个基准测试的性能，并展示了超越传统AutoML的发现能力。
summaries.2603.29640:
机器学习;强化学习  
背景/任务：探索AI能否加速AI自身发展，特别是处理成本高、周期长、弱监督的研究循环。  
做了什么：提出了ASI-Evolve框架，通过进化代理和认知模块，在架构设计、数据管理和算法优化中实现了AI驱动的发现，提升了多项任务性能。
summaries.2604.02190:
计算机科学；自动驾驶  
背景/任务：适应Vision-Language-Action模型于自动驾驶任务时面临空间感知与语义推理的冲突。  
核心工作：提出UniDriveVLA模型，通过专家解耦和混合Transformer架构分离感知与推理，并采用渐进训练策略提升性能。
summaries.2604.00917:
软件工程;软件维护  
背景/任务是研究AI编码代理在开源项目中日益增长的角色及其对软件开发过程的影响。  
研究构建了一个包含约11万条开源拉取请求的数据集，比较了五种主流编码代理的使用差异，并评估了AI生成代码与人类编写代码在长期维护中的表现。
summaries.2603.27666:
计算机视觉;生成模型  
背景/任务：解决云部署扩散模型带来的数据隐私问题，探索适用于边缘设备的可控视觉生成方法。  
所做工作：提出一种基于线性注意力架构的统一门控条件模块，支持多类型条件输入，在多个任务上实现了优于现有方法的生成性能。
summaries.2604.01569:
计算机视觉；多模态学习  
现有视频多模态大语言模型的评测存在分数虚高和缺乏证据验证的缺陷。  
该研究提出了一个分层基准VideoZeroBench，通过五级评估协议揭示模型在长视频问答中基于时空证据推理的严重不足。
summaries.2604.01676:
计算机科学;软件工程  
背景/任务：解决传统RPA的脆弱性和当前视觉语言模型GUI代理的非确定性问题，以实现稳定、可靠的图形用户界面流程自动化。  
所做工作：提出了一种轻量级、基于视觉的通用RPA方法（GPA），通过序贯蒙特卡洛定位和就绪校准来提升鲁棒性与确定性，并以完全本地化执行保障隐私和速度，实验证明其成功率与执行速度优于对比方法。
summaries.2604.01618:
计算机视觉;机器人学  
背景/任务是研究视觉-语言-动作模型在机器人操纵中对抗物理可实现的三维对抗纹理攻击的鲁棒性不足问题。  
他们提出了Tex3D框架，通过前景-背景解耦和轨迹感知对抗优化方法，在仿真和真实机器人环境中实现了对三维对抗纹理的端到端优化，显著降低了模型性能。
summaries.2603.30043:
计算机视觉;生成模型  
研究视频扩散模型在解决迷宫任务中的内部推理机制。  
发现模型早期规划承诺和路径长度主导迷宫难度，并提出了ChEaP方法提升长序列生成性能。
summaries.2603.28068:
计算机视觉；自然语言处理  
背景/任务是评估现有图像生成模型能否为学术论文生成可直接使用的插图，但现有评估方法存在不足。  
他们提出了AIBench基准，通过VQA评估逻辑正确性和VLM评估美学质量，并发现模型在该任务上性能差异显著且逻辑与美学难以同时优化。
summaries.2604.02289:
计算机视觉；图形学  
背景是现有多模态大模型在3D生成上存在数据稀缺和几何不一致的问题。  
他们提出了Omni123模型，通过统一2D和3D生成的训练框架，利用2D数据作为几何先验来提升3D生成质量。
summaries.2604.01666:
计算机视觉；视频生成  
视频扩散模型在生成动态运动和精细可控视频上存在局限，主要因训练数据中此类样本稀缺。  
该研究提出DynaVid框架，利用合成光流数据分阶段生成视频，以合成数据学习动态运动，用真实数据保持视觉真实。
summaries.2604.01014:
人工智能安全; 机器学习隐私与安全  
现有成员推理攻击方法依赖静态启发式规则，难以适配不同大模型，导致性能受限。  
本文提出AutoMIA框架，将成员推理转化为自动化策略探索与进化过程，通过闭环评估生成并优化攻击策略，实现了模型无关的高效攻击。
summaries.2604.02007:
人工智能;强化学习
背景是构建能在多领域进行高效推理的通用模型，面临训练与推理效率的挑战。
研究提出了带自适应采样和难度感知长度惩罚的训练方法，在多个基准上实现了更高精度与更短推理路径。
summaries.2603.28130:
自然语言处理;计算机视觉  
当前文档解析研究集中于少数主流语言的整洁数字文档，缺乏多语言、多场景的评估基准。  
该研究构建了首个多语言文档解析基准MDPBench，涵盖17种语言与多样拍摄条件，评估发现开源模型在非拉丁脚本和真实拍摄文档上性能显著下降。
summaries.2604.01760:
自然语言处理;语音合成  
背景/任务：解决现有自回归神经编解码语言模型在长语音合成中文本条件弱化的问题，提升零样本语音克隆性能。  
所做工作：提出T5Gemma-TTS模型，采用编码器-解码器架构和PM-RoPE技术，在多语言数据上训练，实现了更好的说话人相似度和时长控制。
summaries.2604.01328:
机器学习;优化算法  
传统科学发现依赖人工试错的低效循环，需要更智能的实验设计方法。  
该教程提出用贝叶斯优化框架，通过概率模型和采集函数自动化实验选择，以加速材料、化学等领域的科学发现。
summaries.2604.01298:
自然语言处理；预测模型  
背景/任务：预测供应链中断事件，这是一个从噪音和非结构化数据中学习罕见高影响事件的挑战。  
研究内容：提出了一个训练大语言模型生成校准概率预测的端到端框架，其性能超越包括GPT-5在内的基线模型，并开源了评估数据集。
summaries.2604.02330:
计算机视觉；生成模型  
现有视频扩散模型难以在交互环境中同时控制多个智能体并绑定动作与主体。  
该研究提出ActionParty模型，通过主体状态令牌和空间偏置机制，实现了对多智能体的精确动作控制和状态跟踪。
summaries.2604.01929:
音频生成；深度学习  
音频研究社区依赖开放生成模型作为开发新方法和建立基线的基础工具。  
索尼AI发布了名为Woosh的公开音效基础模型，包括高质量音频编解码器、文本-音频对齐模型以及文本/视频到音频的生成模型，并在评估中展现了优于现有开放模型的性能。
summaries.2604.02088:
计算机视觉；图像生成与编辑  
背景/任务是为图像编辑提供连续滑块式控制，在保持源图像保真度和一致编辑方向的前提下，解决现有方法训练开销大、鲁棒性不足的问题。  
提出了一种无需训练的方法FlowSlider，通过分解并正交化保真项与导向项，仅缩放导向项即可实现平滑、可靠的编辑强度控制。
summaries.2604.00829:
自然语言处理;多模态学习  
背景/任务：将预训练语言模型适配为视觉语言模型时，会因表征偏移和跨模态干扰导致语言能力下降，传统恢复方法通常增加模块，不够高效灵活。  
所做工作：提出无需额外模块的蒸馏方法LinguDistill，通过层间KV缓存共享利用原始语言模型作为教师，在语言密集数据上选择性蒸馏以恢复语言能力，同时保持多模态任务的视觉基础。
summaries.2604.01479:
计算机视觉；三维重建
背景/任务是在稀疏视角下进行三维建模，解决重建保真度与生成合理性之间的固有矛盾。
提出UniRecGen统一框架，通过共享规范空间和分离式协同学习，将前馈重建与扩散生成结合，以生成完整一致的三维模型。
summaries.2603.26233:
自然语言处理；智能体与多智能体系统
背景/任务是：评估大型语言模型智能体在软件工程等开放领域处理模糊指令时的澄清能力。
研究设计了一个不确定性感知的多智能体框架，将模糊性检测与代码执行解耦，显著提升了任务解决率并展现出良好的不确定性校准。
summaries.2603.26259:
信息检索;机器学习  
背景/任务：研究交互式检索模型中潜在的性能瓶颈，特别是长度偏差和相似度分布问题。  
研究内容：分析了双向和因果交互式模型在NanoBEIR基准上的长度偏差，并验证了MaxSim操作符对顶层令牌相似度的高效利用。
summaries.2604.00491:
人工智能系统；并行计算
背景/任务是传统LLM代码生成代理采用串行执行模式导致生成与执行阶段空闲等待，造成端到端延迟问题。  
研究提出了并行执行范式，设计实现Eager系统，通过流水线化生成、检测与执行来显著降低延迟。
summaries.2604.01619:
计算机视觉;生物信息学  
背景/任务：传统形态特征提取依赖专家且效率低，限制了大规模生态研究。  
工作：提出基于稀疏自编码器和视觉语言提示的自动化特征标注流程，构建了大规模昆虫形态特征数据集。
summaries.2604.01152:
机器学习;自然语言处理  
该论文针对大型语言模型在多领域持续微调中的灾难性遗忘和参数爆炸问题，提出了一种模块化架构。  
该架构通过冻结的适配器堆栈、专家混合路由、零遗忘约束和基于结果的元路由器，实现了领域知识的可组合积累与高效复用。
summaries.2604.00356:
人工智能;机器学习  
背景/任务是改进已部署的智能体系统时面临轨迹数据量大、审查成本高的挑战。  
他们提出了一种基于轻量级信号对智能体交互轨迹进行筛选的框架，通过高效识别高信息量的轨迹来优化后续处理效率。
summaries.2603.06679:
计算机科学; 人机交互  
背景/任务是提升视频世界模型的用户控制和共享推理能力。  
它引入了独立的外部记忆模块，将生成分解为记忆、观察和动态模块，以实现可编辑环境和实时多人一致交互。
summaries.2603.24257:
计算机视觉;自然语言处理  
背景/任务是解决视觉语言模型在跨视角下对同一物体描述不一致的问题，这阻碍了具身智能体构建长期一致的语义表示。  
他们提出了一个统一的、带记忆增强的视觉语言智能体，通过自回归框架同时处理数据关联、物体描述和探索策略，并使用自监督方法训练模型以提升描述一致性和性能。
summaries.2603.27449:
计算机视觉；计算机图形学  
背景/任务是解决传统物理模拟在精细接触式人-物交互动作生成中泛化性和可扩展性不足的问题。  
他们提出了LOME，这是一个以自我为中心的世界模型，通过联合估计空间人类动作和环境上下文，在输入图像、文本提示和每帧动作条件下生成逼真的人-物交互视频。
summaries.2604.01404:
自然语言处理;模型可解释性  
背景是研究语言模型回答实体相关事实问题时涉及的内部机制。  
通过定位和干预MLP神经元，验证了早期层的稀疏神经元能以紧凑方式实现实体检索和规范化。
summaries.2603.29042:
自然语言处理；语音识别  
背景/任务是提升跨语言和低资源语音的phone recognition性能，克服现有模型泛化不足的问题。  
作者提出了PhoneticXEUS模型，通过大规模多语言数据训练和系统实验，在多项评测中取得最优结果，并公开了数据和代码。

deep_analysis.2604.02329:
这篇论文的核心内容可以理解为：**为了解决3D图形学中“渲染”和“逆渲染”任务数据不足的问题，作者们从高质量的电子游戏里“挖”出了一个巨大的数据集，并用这个数据集来训练AI模型，让它们更好地理解和生成真实世界的复杂场景。**

下面我为你详细拆解一下：

**(1) 这篇论文所在的任务是什么？**
这篇论文属于**计算机视觉（CV）和计算机图形学（CG）的交叉领域**，具体涉及两个核心的“双向”任务：
*   **任务一：逆渲染**
    *   **输入**：一张或一段真实的RGB图像/视频。
    *   **输出**：分解出构成这张图像的内在物理属性。可以想象成把一张照片“拆解”成它的组成部分，比如：
        *   **深度图**：每个像素离相机有多远。
        *   **法线图**：每个像素所在表面的朝向。
        *   **反照率**：物体表面的基础颜色（排除光照影响）。
        *   **金属度/粗糙度**：描述材质是像金属还是塑料，表面是光滑还是粗糙。
    *   **功能**：让AI理解真实世界的3D结构和物理属性，是数字重建世界的关键一步。
*   **任务二：前向渲染 / 可控生成**
    *   **输入**：上述那些物理属性（比如深度图、法线图等，也叫 **G-buffer**），加上一个文本提示（比如“夕阳下的城市”）。
    *   **输出**：一张符合这些物理约束和文本描述的高质量、逼真的图像或视频。
    *   **功能**：根据给定的“蓝图”（G-buffer）和“要求”（文本），生成或编辑逼真的视觉内容，比如改变游戏场景的风格、天气等。

**(2) 这篇论文想解决什么问题？**
**核心问题：数据瓶颈。**
现有的用于训练这些AI模型的**合成数据集**（比如用Blender等软件生成的）存在严重局限：
*   **不够真实**：场景简单、材质单一、缺少复杂的光照和天气效果（如雾、雨、运动模糊）。
*   **缺乏动态**：很多是静态图片或很短的视频，缺少长时间、连贯的动态场景。
*   **结果**：用这些数据训练的模型，一旦遇到真实世界复杂、动态的视频，就会“翻车”——分解出的属性不准确，生成的视频闪烁、不连贯，无法处理复杂反射、精细植被、动态物体等。

**(3) 论文的核心思路是什么？**
**思路非常直接：既然人工合成的数据不够好，那就去“真实”的虚拟世界——顶级3A游戏里找！**
1.  **造一个强大的数据集**：
    *   **来源**：从《黑神话：悟空》等高质量游戏中采集。
    *   **方法**：开发了一个巧妙的“双屏拼接”采集管道，在不修改游戏的情况下，直接从显卡渲染管线中“拦截”出高质量的G-buffer数据，并同步录制对应的游戏画面。
    *   **规模与质量**：最终得到了**400万帧**连续、高分辨率（720p）、带各种天气和时间效果的视频-G-buffer配对数据。这个数据集的真实性、复杂性和时间连贯性远超以往。
2.  **用新数据训练和评估模型**：
    *   **训练**：用这个新数据集去微调现有的先进模型（如DiffusionRenderer）。给模型“喂”更真实、更复杂的数据，让它学得更好。
    *   **评估**：提出一个新的**基于视觉大模型（VLM）的评估协议**。因为真实世界没有G-buffer的“标准答案”，传统像素级指标（如PSNR）不适用。他们让VLM（如GPT-4V）从**语义正确性、空间准确性和时间一致性**三个维度来评判模型输出，发现这个评判结果与人类的主观评价高度一致。

**(4) 结果怎么样？**
论文通过实验证明了新数据集的价值：
*   **逆渲染效果更好**：用他们数据训练的模型，在分解真实世界视频的深度、法线、材质时，**准确性、鲁棒性和时间稳定性都显著提升**，跨越了“合成数据”到“真实数据”的鸿沟。
*   **前向渲染/编辑能力更强**：他们的模型能够利用G-buffer进行高度可控的内容生成。例如，用户可以通过输入文本提示（如“让它看起来像水墨画”），直接改变一个游戏场景的整体艺术风格，效果非常逼真且符合物理约束。
*   **评估方法有效**：提出的VLM评估方法与人类判断高度相关，为在没有真实G-buffer的情况下评估逆渲染模型提供了可靠的新工具。

**简单总结：**
这篇论文可以看作是为3D视觉和图形AI模型打造了一个“终极训练营”。通过从顶级游戏中采集海量、高质量、高动态的数据，他们**解决了该领域长期存在的数据荒问题**，使得AI模型在理解和生成复杂、动态的真实世界场景方面获得了质的飞跃。同时，他们提出的VLM评估方法也巧妙地解决了真实场景下的模型评测难题。
deep_analysis.2604.01001:
这篇论文是关于一个叫 **EgoSim** 的“自我中心世界模拟器”。我来用简单的话给你解释一下它的核心内容。

### (1) 这篇论文所在的任务是什么？
*   **任务名称**：**自我中心交互式视频生成与3D世界状态模拟**。
*   **任务输入**：
    1.  **一个初始的自我中心视角的图片**（就是你第一人称看到的场景）。
    2.  **一个动作序列**（比如手或机器人的一系列抓取、移动等操作指令）。
*   **任务输出**：
    1.  **一段逼真的自我中心视角视频**，展示你输入的动作如何与3D场景进行交互（比如手拿起一个杯子）。
    2.  **一个被持续更新的3D场景状态**（比如杯子被拿走后，它在3D空间里的新位置被记录下来）。
*   **任务功能**：它就像一个“第一人称的游戏引擎”。你给出“做什么”（动作），它就能预测并生成“你会看到什么”（视频），并且会记住世界因此发生了哪些**持久性变化**，从而可以进行连续、多步的交互模拟。

### (2) 这篇论文想解决什么问题？
现有的类似技术有两个主要缺陷：
1.  **缺乏3D空间一致性**：有些方法生成的视频，当视角（摄像机）移动时，场景里的物体结构会“漂移”或变形，不符合真实3D世界的物理规律。
2.  **世界状态是静态的**：有些方法虽然能构建3D场景，但把它当作不变的背景板。当你与物体交互后（比如推开一扇门），系统不会记住“门现在是开着的”这个新状态，下次模拟时门可能还是关的，无法进行连续、逻辑连贯的交互。

**简单说，就是以前的模拟器要么“画面会穿帮”，要么“记性不好”。**

### (3) 论文的核心思路是什么？
EgoSim 的核心思路是 **“用可编辑的3D点云作为世界的记忆，并不断更新它”**。它把整个过程分成了两步：

1.  **第一步：基于当前世界状态的视频生成**
    *   先把初始画面里的手“抹掉”，重建一个**静态3D场景的点云**（可以理解为这个场景的3D骨架/记忆）。
    *   然后，模型根据你输入的动作和当前摄像机的自我运动，像“渲染引擎”一样，从这个3D点云“记忆”中，生成出第一人称视角的交互视频。这保证了画面在3D空间中是**一致**的。

2.  **第二步：交互感知的世界状态更新**（**这是论文的关键创新**）
    *   视频生成后，场景其实已经变了（比如杯子被挪了位置）。
    *   系统会分析生成的视频，**识别出哪些物体被交互了**（比如那个杯子），然后根据它在视频里的新样子，去**更新3D点云“记忆”中对应部分的状态**（把点云中杯子的位置挪到新地方）。
    *   这样，世界状态就**被更新了**。下一次模拟时，系统会基于这个**更新后的点云**来生成视频，从而实现连续、状态持久的模拟。

**另外一个大贡献是数据**：为了训练这个模型，他们设计了一个自动化的流水线，从海量的、网上的单视角第一人称视频里，自动提取出3D点云、摄像机路径和动作，解决了训练数据稀缺的瓶颈。

### (4) 结果怎么样？
论文通过大量实验证明，EgoSim 在多个方面都显著优于之前的方法：
*   **视觉质量更高**：生成的交互视频更清晰、更逼真。
*   **空间一致性更好**：即使在摄像机大幅度运动下，场景物体也不会扭曲或漂移。
*   **泛化能力更强**：能处理更复杂的场景和灵巧的交互动作（比如组装物品）。
*   **支持跨载体迁移**：用人类动作数据训练出来的模型，可以较好地迁移到控制机器人手臂进行模拟，展示了在机器人训练中的应用潜力。
*   **消融实验**：验证了他们提出的“状态更新模块”和数据处理流水线都非常有效。

**总结一下**：EgoSim 提出了一个像“有记忆的第一人称游戏引擎”的模拟器。它用**可更新的3D点云**作为世界的持久化状态，既能生成空间一致的交互视频，又能记住每次交互带来的改变，从而实现了真正意义上的、连续互动的世界模拟。同时，他们用创新的数据构建方法，让模型能从海量网络数据中学习，能力更强。
deep_analysis.2604.02289:
这篇论文的核心内容可以用一个简单的比喻来理解：**想象有一个AI，它不仅能根据你的文字描述画出一张图，还能直接根据文字“捏”出一个3D模型。这篇论文就是想让AI学会“捏3D”这个更高级的技能，但它遇到了一个大问题——没有足够多的3D模型给它学习。**

下面我来为你详细拆解一下：

### (1) 任务是什么？
*   **任务名称：** 文本到3D的生成与编辑。它包含两个主要子任务：
    1.  **文本到3D生成：** 你输入一句话（如“一只戴着帽子的小猫”），模型直接输出一个对应的3D模型。
    2.  **指令式3D编辑：** 你在一个已有的3D模型上，用文字指令（如“给这个角色加上一件斗篷”）对它进行修改，模型直接输出修改后的新3D模型。
*   **任务输入：** 一段描述性的文字。
*   **任务输出：** 一个3D模型（通常表示为点云、网格或神经辐射场等）。
*   **任务功能：** 让AI具备**原生**理解和生成3D世界的能力，就像现在的AI能原生理解和生成图片和文字一样。这对于创建虚拟世界、游戏资产、机器人仿真环境等至关重要。

### (2) 想解决什么问题？
**核心问题：数据严重不足。**
*   **“富2D，穷3D”：** 互联网上有海量的图片和文字配对数据，可以轻松训练出强大的文生图模型。但高质量的3D模型数据非常稀少且制作成本高。
*   **现有方法的缺陷：** 为了绕过3D数据少的问题，当前主流方法是“曲线救国”：
    1.  先用文生图模型生成多角度的图片。
    2.  再用一个复杂的优化算法，把这些图片“粘”成一个3D模型。
    *   **缺点：** 这个过程慢、计算成本高，而且图片之间可能对不上，导致生成的3D模型几何结构扭曲、不一致。

这篇论文想解决的就是**如何用有限的3D数据，训练出一个能直接、快速、高质量地从文字生成3D的“原生3D大模型”**。

### (3) 核心思路是什么？
**核心思想：用海量的2D图片知识，来“辅导”3D能力的学习。**

作者提出了一个名为 **Omni123** 的模型，其核心创新点如下：

1.  **统一框架：** 把文字、图片、3D模型都“翻译”成同一种语言——**离散的符号序列（Token）**。这样，一个Transformer模型就能同时处理生成图片和生成3D两种任务。
2.  **交叉模态循环训练：** 这是论文最关键的思路。模型不是单纯地学“文字→3D”，而是进行一种**循环训练**，例如：
    *   **文字 → 图片 → 3D**：先根据文字生成一张图，再根据这张图生成3D。这样，2D图片作为中间桥梁，提供了丰富的视觉和几何线索。
    *   **3D → 图片**：给定一个3D模型，生成它的多个视角图片。这强迫模型理解3D的几何结构，以保证生成的多视角图片是自洽的。
    *   通过这种在“语义（文字）-视觉（图片）-几何（3D）”之间的循环学习，模型学到的3D表示必须同时满足**语义正确、看起来逼真、几何结构合理**这三个条件。
3.  **利用不完美的数据：** 它不需要大量完美的“文字-图片-3D”三件套数据。而是混合使用各种配对数据（文字-图片、图片-3D、文字-3D），通过上述循环训练方式，让知识在不同任务间流动和加强。

**简单来说，就是让模型在学3D的时候，不断被2D图片“考问”和“纠正”，从而用丰富的2D知识弥补3D数据的不足，学会更靠谱的3D生成。**

### (4) 结果怎么样？
根据论文中的实验，Omni123模型取得了不错的效果：
*   **在文本生成3D任务上：** 相比之前的“曲线救国”方法，Omni123生成的3D模型**几何一致性更好**（更少扭曲和断裂），并且**与文字描述的语义对齐更准确**。
*   **在指令编辑3D任务上：** 能够根据文字指令，直接对3D模型进行有效的修改（比如给角色换衣服、加道具），而无需经过复杂的2D优化流程。
*   **验证了思路：** 实验结果支持了他们的核心假设——**将2D和3D生成统一在一个框架内进行交叉训练，确实是利用有限3D数据构建强大3D生成模型的一条有效路径。**

**总结一下：** 这篇论文就像在教AI“捏橡皮泥”（做3D），但橡皮泥（3D数据）很少。于是他们想了个办法：先让AI大量地“看画册”（2D图片），学习物体应该长什么样；然后让AI练习“照着画捏泥巴”（图片→3D），以及“把泥巴拍成照片”（3D→图片）。通过这种反复练习，AI最终学会了只凭一段文字描述，就能捏出像模像样的3D作品。Omni123就是实现这个想法的第一个成功案例。
deep_analysis.2604.01479:
你好！我来用简单的语言为你解读一下这篇论文。

**(1) 任务是什么？**

*   **任务名称：** 从稀疏视图（少数几张照片）重建3D物体。
*   **任务输入：** 一个物体的**几张（比如2-4张）不同角度的照片**（可能不知道相机的精确位置）。
*   **任务输出：** 一个**完整的、细节丰富的、高精度的3D模型**（论文中提到了点云和网格）。
*   **任务功能/目标：** 只凭少数几张照片，就能“脑补”出这个物体的完整3D形状，而且既要像照片里的真实物体，又要符合现实中这类物体该有的合理结构。

**(2) 想解决什么问题？**

这个任务存在一个根本矛盾，就像“鱼和熊掌”：
*   **传统3D重建方法（“抄”）：** 能精确还原照片里看到的部分，**忠实度高**。但因为照片太少，看不到的地方就一片空白，导致**模型不完整**，而且缺乏对物体整体结构的理解。
*   **新兴3D生成方法（“猜”）：** 利用AI学习到的海量物体知识（先验），能“脑补”出完整、合理的3D模型，**完整性好**。但它容易“放飞自我”，添加一些照片里没有的细节（幻觉），导致**不忠实**于输入的具体物体。

这篇论文想解决的问题就是：**如何把“忠实但残缺”的重建方法，和“完整但可能瞎猜”的生成方法结合起来，得到一个既忠实又完整的3D模型。**

**(3) 核心思路是什么？**

论文提出了一个叫 **“UniRecGen”** 的统一框架。核心思想是：**让“重建模块”和“生成模块”分工合作，而不是让它们互相打架。**

他们遇到了两大技术难题：
1.  **训练方式不合：** 重建是“一步到位”的确定性训练，生成是“逐步去噪”的随机性训练，直接放一起训练会非常不稳定。
2.  **语言不通：** 重建模块通常在照片相关的坐标系里工作，输出具体的点；生成模块通常在一种抽象的、物体中心的“潜空间”里工作。两者信息无法直接交流。

**他们的解决方案很巧妙：**

*   **分步训练，模块化合作（解决难题1）：**
    *   **第一步：** 先单独训练好**重建模块**。它的任务是根据输入照片，快速生成一个粗糙但**忠实**的3D点云草图。这个草图叫做 **“几何锚点”** ，它确定了物体大致的形状和位置。
    *   **第二步：** 固定住重建模块，再训练**生成模块**。生成模块的任务是：**以“几何锚点”为指引**，去生成一个完整、细腻的3D模型。这样，生成过程就被“锚定”在了真实输入的基础上，不会乱猜。

*   **建立“通用语”和“翻译官”（解决难题2）：**
    *   他们设计了一个**共享的规范坐标系**，就像给两个模块建立了一个共同的“工作台”。
    *   然后，他们修改了重建模块，让它输出的“几何锚点”直接生在这个共享工作台上。
    *   接着，他们设计了一种 **“潜增强多视图条件”** 机制。简单说，就是不仅把“几何锚点”（点云）送给生成模块看，还把重建过程中提取的一些中间特征也一起送过去。这相当于给了生成模块更丰富的“上下文提示”，告诉它：“看，这里是照片确认过的结构，那些空白的地方请你根据常识合理补全。”

**打个比方：**
就像画一幅肖像画。
*   **重建模块** 像一位**速写画家**，快速根据几张照片勾勒出人物的**轮廓和关键五官**（几何锚点），非常像本人，但只是线稿，不完整。
*   **生成模块** 像一位**渲染大师**，他拿到速写线稿后，**基于对人体结构的深刻理解**，在线稿的基础上补充头发、皮肤质感、衣物褶皱等细节，最终画出一幅**既像本人又完整细腻**的油画。

**(4) 结果怎么样？**

根据论文中的实验：
*   **效果更好：** UniRecGen 在从稀疏视图重建3D物体的任务上，**效果超过了当时其他先进的方法**。它能生成更完整、更一致（不同角度看起来和谐）、且更忠实于输入照片的3D模型。
*   **更鲁棒：** 因为有了“几何锚点”的强力约束，整个系统对于输入照片的数量和质量变化**更加稳定可靠**。

**总结一下：**
这篇论文的核心贡献是提出了一种**“先锚定，后生成”** 的协同框架，通过**模块化设计**和**共享空间对齐**，巧妙地融合了3D重建的“忠实性”和3D生成的“完整性与先验知识”，最终实现了从少数图片得到高质量3D模型的目标。这种思路清晰有效，具有很强的启发性。
deep_analysis.2603.06679:
好的，没问题。我们一步步来拆解这篇论文，我会用尽量简单直白的语言来解释。

### (1) 这篇论文所在的任务是什么？

这篇论文属于 **“交互式视频世界模型”** 或更具体地说 **“扩散游戏引擎”** 的研究领域。

*   **任务输入**：
    1.  **动作指令**：比如玩家按下“前进”、“左转”、“开火”等按键。
    2.  **世界状态**（论文的核心创新）：一个可以编辑和共享的**外部记忆模块**，里面存储着世界的“蓝图”，比如一张顶视角的2D地图（包含了墙壁、房间布局等信息），以及所有玩家的位置、状态等。

*   **任务输出**：
    *   **下一帧图像**：根据玩家的动作和当前世界的状态，实时生成第一人称视角（就像在玩《Doom》这类游戏一样）的游戏画面。

*   **任务功能**：
    *   它不是一个简单的视频生成器，而是一个可以**被玩家实时交互、影响和探索的虚拟世界模拟器**。你可以把它想象成一个用AI模型驱动的“游戏引擎”，它负责根据规则（学习自数据）和玩家输入，来渲染画面和更新世界。

### (2) 这篇论文想解决什么问题？

这篇论文主要想解决现有AI游戏引擎的两个大问题：

1.  **可控性与可编辑性差**：之前的模型（如Genie, Game Factory等）生成世界时，其“内部状态”是隐式的、黑箱的。这意味着：
    *   **你无法设计关卡**：你不能像游戏设计师一样，提前画好一张地图（比如一个迷宫），然后让AI在这个地图里生成游戏。
    *   **体验难以复现**：同一个初始画面，你两次玩可能生成完全不同的世界，无法保证一致性。
    *   **长期控制困难**：玩久了，AI生成的内容可能会“跑偏”，脱离你最初的意图。

2.  **不支持真正的多人游戏**：之前的模型本质上是**单人体验**。即使有多个AI角色，它们也没有一个**共享的、一致的世界状态**。想象一下玩《我的世界》联机，但你和朋友看到的是两个完全不同的、互不影响的世界，这显然不是真正的多人游戏。

**核心问题根源**：作者认为，问题的关键在于缺少一个 **“显式的外部记忆”** 。现有的模型只在内部处理信息（类似短期记忆），而没有一个可以持久存储、能被所有玩家共同读写和编辑的“长期记忆”或“世界数据库”。

### (3) 论文的核心思路是什么？

为了解决上面的问题，作者提出了一个**模块化**的架构，把复杂的生成任务分解成三个部分：

1.  **记忆模块**：这就是论文的核心——**外部记忆**。它是一个独立于生成模型的“数据库”，里面存着一张可编辑的**2D顶视角地图（Minimap）** 和所有玩家的状态。这张地图就是世界的“蓝图”，用户可以直接在上面画画来设计关卡。

2.  **观测模块**：这是一个**扩散模型**，负责“画图”。它的任务是：**根据记忆模块提供的地图信息、玩家当前位置和动作，再结合最近几帧的画面，生成下一帧的第一人称视角图像**。它只负责“看到什么”，不负责“世界怎么变”。

3.  **动态模块**：这是一个**轻量级的Transformer模型**，负责“更新世界”。它的任务是：**根据玩家的动作和当前的观测（画面），计算出玩家的新位置、是否击中敌人等状态变化，然后去更新记忆模块里的数据**（比如把玩家位置从A点移动到B点）。

**工作流程（像一个游戏引擎循环）**：
*   **开始**：用户先编辑好记忆模块里的2D地图（设计关卡）。
*   **循环**：
    1.  **观测模块** 读取记忆（地图和玩家位置），生成当前玩家看到的画面。
    2.  玩家输入动作（如“前进”）。
    3.  **动态模块** 根据动作和画面，计算出新状态（玩家移动了）。
    4.  **更新记忆模块**（把玩家的新位置存进去）。
    5.  回到第1步，生成下一帧。

**这个设计的妙处**：
*   **支持关卡设计**：因为地图是显式的、可编辑的，用户可以直接画地图来控制世界结构。
*   **支持多人游戏**：多个玩家共享同一个记忆模块。玩家A的行动被动态模块计算后，会更新到共享记忆里。当玩家B的观测模块生成画面时，它会读取这个更新后的记忆，因此能看到玩家A造成的变化（比如门被打开了，敌人被打倒了），从而实现**一致且相互影响的多人体验**。

### (4) 结果怎么样？

论文在《Doom》游戏环境中进行了实验，并展示了两个主要应用的结果：

1.  **关卡设计应用**：
    *   **效果**：用户可以画一个简单的2D草图（比如几个房间和走廊），模型能生成出符合这个布局的、可探索的3D第一人称游戏场景。生成的场景在几何结构上与用户输入的地图高度一致。
    *   **评估**：在定量指标（如布局匹配度）和用户研究中，他们的方法都优于没有显式记忆的基线模型。

2.  **多人交互应用**：
    *   **效果**：两个玩家可以同时进入同一个由AI生成的世界。实验显示，一个玩家的行动（如开枪击败怪物）会实时反映在另一个玩家的视角里，世界状态保持一致。
    *   **评估**：通过对比“共享记忆”和“不共享记忆”的版本，证明了共享记忆对于保持跨视角一致性至关重要。用户也认为共享记忆的版本体验更连贯、更像真正的多人游戏。

**总结一下**：这篇论文通过引入 **“显式外部记忆”** 和 **“模块化架构”** ，成功地让AI驱动的游戏世界变得更**可控**（可设计关卡）和**可共享**（支持多人联机），为未来更复杂、更开放的交互式AI世界模拟迈出了重要的一步。

2604.02329.caption: Figure 1:We present a large-scale dataset curated from game engines to support scalable generative world rendering.The dataset provides high-resolution RGB videos with aligned G-buffers, covering continuous and dynamic scenes, long temporal trajectories, and diverse visual conditions.
2604.02329.caption_zh: 图 1：我们提出了一个从游戏引擎中整理的大规模数据集，以支持可扩展的生成式世界渲染。该数据集提供高分辨率 RGB 视频及对齐的 G-buffer，涵盖连续动态场景、长时序轨迹以及多样化的视觉条件。

2604.02329.caption:
Figure 2:Motivation.Existing approaches, such as DiffusionRenderer, are primarily trained on synthetic datasets, struggling to capture (a) complex reflection and illumination effects, (b) real scene elements (e.g., humans and cars), (c) fine-grained visual details, (d) dynamic motion, and (e) long-range temporal dependencies in long video sequences.
By contrast, the proposed dataset provides high-fidelity, scene-level supervision with rich geometry, appearance, and temporal dynamics, enabling scalable generative video inverse rendering that generalizes more effectively to real-world scenarios.
2604.02329.caption_zh: 图 2：研究动机。现有方法（如 DiffusionRenderer）主要在合成数据集上训练，难以捕捉：（a）复杂的反射与光照效果，（b）真实场景元素（如人与车辆），（c）细粒度的视觉细节，（d）动态运动，以及（e）长视频序列中的长程时序依赖性。相比之下，本数据集提供了高保真、场景级的监督信息，包含丰富的几何、外观与时序动态，从而支持可扩展的生成式视频逆向渲染，并能更有效地泛化至真实世界场景。

2604.02329.caption:
Figure 3:Pipeline.Stage I: We curate video sequences containing RGB frames and five corresponding G-buffer channels from commercial game engines. Buffer interception is performed using ReShade, which allows us to capture intermediate rendering outputs at runtime. Because a single rendering pass exposes thousands of heterogeneous and largely irrelevant buffers, an automated filtering procedure is required to identify valid candidates.
To disambiguate target G-buffers from irrelevant render targets, we utilize RenderDoc for offline inspection to define filtering rules based on metadata invariants. By manually validating the semantic accuracy of the remaining buffers, we iteratively optimize these rules into robust signatures that ensure consistent runtime identification. As a final verification step, we re-render the RGB frames from the collected G-buffers using a deferred shading pipeline and check for pixel-level consistency with the original RGB outputs.Stage II: We annotate meta-information for each sequence and filter out unsatisfactory frames based on quality criteria.Stage III: We synthesize motion blur to enhance temporal realism and better match real-world capture conditions.
2604.02329.caption_zh: 图 3：流程概述。第一阶段：我们从商业游戏引擎中整理包含 RGB 帧及五个对应 G-buffer 通道的视频序列。缓冲区截取通过 ReShade 实现，该工具允许我们在运行时捕获中间渲染输出。由于单次渲染过程会暴露数千个异构且大多无关的缓冲区，因此需要自动过滤程序来识别有效候选。为从无关渲染目标中明确目标 G-buffer，我们使用 RenderDoc 进行离线检查，基于元数据不变性定义过滤规则。通过人工验证剩余缓冲区的语义准确性，我们迭代优化这些规则为鲁棒的特征标识，以确保在运行时能一致识别。作为最终验证步骤，我们使用延迟着色管线从收集的 G-buffer 重新渲染 RGB 帧，并检查其与原始 RGB 输出的像素级一致性。第二阶段：我们为每个序列标注元信息，并依据质量标准过滤不合格帧。第三阶段：我们合成运动模糊以增强时序真实感，并更好地匹配真实世界拍摄条件。

2604.01001.caption: Figure 1:Given a scene image and a sequence of actions,EgoSimgenerates temporally and spatially consistent egocentric observations and high-quality dexterous interactions. Egosim also persistently updates the 3D scene state for continuous simulation. We propose a data construction pipeline to leverage web-scale egocentric video data, and thus strengthen the generalization ability of Egosim with scalable scene-interaction pairs. EgoSim also exhibits strong few-shot adaptation capability to real-world scenarios and diverse robotic embodiments.
2604.01001.caption_zh: 图1：给定场景图像与动作序列，EgoSim 能够生成具有时空一致性的第一人称视角观测结果以及高质量灵巧交互。EgoSim 还持续更新3D场景状态以实现连续仿真。我们提出一种数据构建流程，以利用网络规模的第一人称视频数据，从而通过可扩展的场景-交互对增强 EgoSim 的泛化能力。EgoSim 还展现出对真实世界场景及多样化机器人具身的强大少样本适应能力。

2604.01001.caption: Figure 2:Overview of EgoSim. Our framework enables continuous egocentric simulation via updatable world modeling. It consists of (1) a Geometry-action-aware Observation Simulation model that synthesizes action-conditioned visual dynamicsOkO_{k}based on current scene geometry; and (2) an Interaction-aware state updating module that tracks point-based interaction-aware object states from generated observations and integrates them back into the persistent 3D world state to updateSkS_{k}.
2604.01001.caption_zh: 图2：EgoSim 概览。本框架通过可更新的世界建模实现连续的第一人称视角仿真。它包括：(1) 几何-动作感知的观测仿真模型，该模型基于当前场景几何合成以动作为条件的视觉动态 $O_{k}$；以及 (2) 交互感知状态更新模块，该模块从生成的观测中跟踪基于点的交互感知物体状态，并将其整合回持久的3D世界状态中以更新 $S_{k}$。

2604.01001.caption: Figure 3:Implementation details of Interaction-aware State Updating.State Reconstruction (left) builds a point cloud from observations via modified VIPE. Object State Update (top-right) identifies and tracks interactive objects with a VLM and SAM3, compositing their latest geometry into the scene. Incremental State Fusion (bottom) aligns and merges consecutive states via TSDF fusion.
2604.01001.caption_zh: 图3：交互感知状态更新的实现细节。状态重建（左）通过改进的 VIPE 从观测数据构建点云。物体状态更新（右上）利用 VLM 和 SAM3 识别并跟踪交互物体，并将它们的最新几何结构合成到场景中。增量状态融合（下）通过 TSDF 融合对齐并合并连续状态。

2604.02289.caption: Figure 1:Comparison of Text-to-3D paradigms
2604.02289.caption_zh: 图1：文本到3D生成范式的对比

2604.02289.caption: Figure 2:Omni123enables native 3D generation and editing within a unified multimodal framework trained with limited 3D data. Top: Text-to-joint-2D-and-3D generation results across diverse prompts. Each example shows the generated 2D image (inset) alongside the normal map of the corresponding 3D output, demonstrating high geometric fidelity and strong semantic alignment with the input text. Bottom: Native 3D editing of 3D models through sequential and branching text instructions (e.g., “+wearing a kimono,” “+riding a skateboard”), illustrating the model’s ability to perform iterative and diverse 3D modifications directly in 3D space. More qualitative results are shown in Figures8and9.
2604.02289.caption_zh: 图2：Omni123 在基于有限3D数据训练的统一多模态框架内，实现了原生3D生成与编辑。上图：针对多样化提示的文本到联合-2D-与-3D 生成结果。每个示例展示了生成的2D图像（插图）以及对应3D输出的法线贴图，体现了高几何保真度以及与输入文本的强语义对齐。下图：通过顺序和分支文本指令（例如“+穿着和服”、“+滑滑板”）对3D模型进行的原生3D编辑，展示了模型直接在3D空间中进行迭代且多样化3D修改的能力。更多定性结果见图8和图9。

2604.02289.caption:
Figure 3:Overview of theOmni123architecture. Text is encoded by dual text encoders (CLIP[60]and Qwen3-0.6B[87]) and fed into a conditioning stream, while images and 3D shapes are tokenized into 1D discrete tokens and concatenated into a unified generation stream. The unified autoregressive
transformer backbone uses 24 dual-stream blocks to jointly process the conditioning and generation tokens under causal attention, followed by 6 single-stream layers operating only on generation tokens, and finally with modality-specific linear heads decoding token logits over the 2D and 3D codebooks.
2604.02289.caption_zh: 图3：Omni123 架构概览。文本通过双文本编码器（CLIP[60] 与 Qwen3-0.6B[87]）编码并输入到条件流中，而图像和3D形状则被分词化为1D离散令牌并拼接至统一的生成流中。统一的自回归 Transformer 主干网络使用24个双流模块在因果注意力下联合处理条件和生成令牌，随后是6个仅作用于生成令牌的单流层，最后通过特定模态的线性头在2D和3D码本上解码令牌逻辑值。

2604.01479.caption: Figure 1.UniRecGen enables high-fidelity 3D object reconstruction from sparse, unposed images.It first establishes a deterministic point-cloud “geometric anchor” within a canonical coordinate system. Guided by this anchor, a generative model synthesizes a detailed mesh that preserves instance-specific structural features while producing plausible geometric completions for unobserved regions, ensuring high alignment with the ground-truth shape.
2604.01479.caption_zh: 图1. UniRecGen 能够从稀疏、无位姿图像中进行高保真三维物体重建。该方法首先在规范坐标系中建立一个确定性点云“几何锚点”。在此锚点引导下，生成模型合成出具有细节的网格，该网格在保留实例特异性结构特征的同时，为未观测区域生成合理的几何补全，从而确保与真实形状高度对齐。

2604.01479.caption: Figure 2.Overview of our method.GivenNNunposed input views, we first canonicalize feed-forward multi-view geometry predictions via branch repurposing and similarity alignment to obtain a canonical point cloud (top). We then train a controllable 3D generator conditioned on this point cloud together with multi-view image features, geometry latents, and camera embeddings to synthesize a high-fidelity mesh (bottom).
2604.01479.caption_zh: 图2. 方法概览。给定 N 张无位姿输入视图，我们首先通过分支复用与相似性对齐对前馈多视图几何预测进行规范化，以获得规范点云（上部分）。随后，我们训练一个以该点云、多视图图像特征、几何隐变量及相机嵌入为条件的可控三维生成器，以合成高保真网格（下部分）。

2604.01479.caption: Figure 3.Qualitative Comparison of Canonical Alignment.Our strategy achieves better geometric quality while aligning the canonical object space.
2604.01479.caption_zh: 图3. 规范对齐的定性比较。我们的策略在将物体空间规范对齐的同时，实现了更好的几何质量。

2603.06679.caption: Figure 1:Level Design via Editable Memory.Users define a level through coarse 2D geometry (left). During inference, the diffusion model generates first-person observations consistent with the top-down level layout (right).
2603.06679.caption_zh: 图1：通过可编辑记忆的关卡设计。用户通过粗略的二维几何图形（左）定义关卡。在推理过程中，扩散模型生成与俯视关卡布局一致的第一人称观测图像（右）。

2603.06679.caption: Figure 2:Method overview.We introduce an explicit external memory and factor the diffusion game engine into three modules:Memory(map geometry and pose;Sec.˜3.1),Observation(next-frame generation conditioned on history and memory readouts;Sec.˜3.2), andDynamics(pose update for state progression;Sec.˜3.3).
2603.06679.caption_zh: 图2：方法概述。我们引入了一个显式外部记忆，并将扩散游戏引擎分解为三个模块：记忆（地图几何与姿态；见第3.1节）、观测（基于历史与记忆读取信息生成下一帧；见第3.2节）和动态（用于状态推进的姿态更新；见第3.3节）。

2603.06679.caption: Figure 3:Example rollouts under an authored map and action sequence. Top: minimapMMwith poseptp_{t}(red arrow). Middle: generated first-person observationso^t\hat{o}_{t}. Bottom: actionsata_{t}. The viewpoint evolves coherently with the action inputs while adhering with the designed layout.
2603.06679.caption_zh: 图3：在编写的地图和动作序列下的示例推演轨迹。顶部：包含姿态 p_t（红色箭头）的小地图 MM。中部：生成的第一人称观测图像 ô_t。底部：动作 a_t。视点随动作输入连贯演进，同时遵循设计的布局。

2603.26164.abstract: Data-centric training has emerged as a promising direction for improving large language models (LLMs) by optimizing not only model parameters but also the selection, composition, and weighting of training data during optimization. However, existing approaches to data selection, data mixture optimization, and data reweighting are often developed in isolated codebases with inconsistent interfaces, hindering reproducibility, fair comparison, and practical integration. In this paper, we present DataFlex, a unified data-centric dynamic training framework built upon LLaMA-Factory. DataFlex supports three major paradigms of dynamic data optimization: sample selection, domain mixture adjustment, and sample reweighting, while remaining fully compatible with the original training workflow. It provides extensible trainer abstractions and modular components, enabling a drop-in replacement for standard LLM training, and unifies key model-dependent operations such as embedding extraction, inference, and gradient computation, with support for large-scale settings including DeepSpeed ZeRO-3. We conduct comprehensive experiments across multiple data-centric methods. Dynamic data selection consistently outperforms static full-data training on MMLU across both Mistral-7B and Llama-3.2-3B. For data mixture, DoReMi and ODM improve both MMLU accuracy and corpus-level perplexity over default proportions when pretraining Qwen2.5-1.5B on SlimPajama at 6B and 30B token scales. DataFlex also achieves consistent runtime improvements over original implementations. These results demonstrate that DataFlex provides an effective, efficient, and reproducible infrastructure for data-centric dynamic training of LLMs.

2604.02029.abstract: Latent space is rapidly emerging as a native substrate for language-based models. While modern systems are still commonly understood through explicit token-level generation, an increasing body of work shows that many critical internal processes are more naturally carried out in continuous latent space than in human-readable verbal traces. This shift is driven by the structural limitations of explicit-space computation, including linguistic redundancy, discretization bottlenecks, sequential inefficiency, and semantic loss. This survey aims to provide a unified and up-to-date landscape of latent space in language-based models. We organize the survey into five sequential perspectives: Foundation, Evolution, Mechanism, Ability, and Outlook. We begin by delineating the scope of latent space, distinguishing it from explicit or verbal space and from the latent spaces commonly studied in generative visual models. We then trace the field's evolution from early exploratory efforts to the current large-scale expansion. To organize the technical landscape, we examine existing work through the complementary lenses of mechanism and ability. From the perspective of Mechanism, we identify four major lines of development: Architecture, Representation, Computation, and Optimization. From the perspective of Ability, we show how latent space supports a broad capability spectrum spanning Reasoning, Planning, Modeling, Perception, Memory, Collaboration, and Embodiment. Beyond consolidation, we discuss the key open challenges, and outline promising directions for future research. We hope this survey serves not only as a reference for existing work, but also as a foundation for understanding latent space as a general computational and systems paradigm for next-generation intelligence.

2604.02329.abstract: Scaling generative inverse and forward rendering to real-world scenarios is bottlenecked by the limited realism and temporal coherence of existing synthetic datasets. To bridge this persistent domain gap, we introduce a large-scale, dynamic dataset curated from visually complex AAA games. Using a novel dual-screen stitched capture method, we extracted 4M continuous frames (720p/30 FPS) of synchronized RGB and five G-buffer channels across diverse scenes, visual effects, and environments, including adverse weather and motion-blur variants. This dataset uniquely advances bidirectional rendering: enabling robust in-the-wild geometry and material decomposition, and facilitating high-fidelity G-buffer-guided video generation. Furthermore, to evaluate the real-world performance of inverse rendering without ground truth, we propose a novel VLM-based assessment protocol measuring semantic, spatial, and temporal consistency. Experiments demonstrate that inverse renderers fine-tuned on our data achieve superior cross-dataset generalization and controllable generation, while our VLM evaluation strongly correlates with human judgment. Combined with our toolkit, our forward renderer enables users to edit styles of AAA games from G-buffers using text prompts.
2604.02329.comments: Project page: this https URL

2604.02268.abstract: Agent skills, structured packages of procedural knowledge and executable resources that agents dynamically load at inference time, have become a reliable mechanism for augmenting LLM agents. Yet inference-time skill augmentation is fundamentally limited: retrieval noise introduces irrelevant guidance, injected skill content imposes substantial token overhead, and the model never truly acquires the knowledge it merely follows. We ask whether skills can instead be internalized into model parameters, enabling zero-shot autonomous behavior without any runtime skill retrieval. We introduce SKILL0, an in-context reinforcement learning framework designed for skill internalization. SKILL0 introduces a training-time curriculum that begins with full skill context and progressively withdraws it. Skills are grouped offline by category and rendered with interaction history into a compact visual context, teaching he model tool invocation and multi-turn task completion. A Dynamic Curriculum then evaluates each skill file's on-policy helpfulness, retaining only those from which the current policy still benefits within a linearly decaying budget, until the agent operates in a fully zero-shot setting. Extensive agentic experiments demonstrate that SKILL0 achieves substantial improvements over the standard RL baseline (+9.7\% for ALFWorld and +6.6\% for Search-QA), while maintaining a highly efficient context of fewer than 0.5k tokens per step. Our code is available atthis https URL.

2604.02327.abstract: Pretrained Vision Transformers (ViTs) such as DINOv2 and MAE provide generic image features that can be applied to a variety of downstream tasks such as retrieval, classification, and segmentation. However, such representations tend to focus on the most salient visual cues in the image, with no way to direct them toward less prominent concepts of interest. In contrast, Multimodal LLMs can be guided with textual prompts, but the resulting representations tend to be language-centric and lose their effectiveness for generic visual tasks. To address this, we introduce Steerable Visual Representations, a new class of visual representations, whose global and local features can be steered with natural language. While most vision-language models (e.g., CLIP) fuse text with visual features after encoding (late fusion), we inject text directly into the layers of the visual encoder (early fusion) via lightweight cross-attention. We introduce benchmarks for measuring representational steerability, and demonstrate that our steerable visual features can focus on any desired objects in an image while preserving the underlying representation quality. Our method also matches or outperforms dedicated approaches on anomaly detection and personalized object discrimination, exhibiting zero-shot generalization to out-of-distribution tasks.

2604.01658.abstract: Large language model (LLM)-based evolution is a promising approach for open-ended discovery, where progress requires sustained search and knowledge accumulation. Existing methods still rely heavily on fixed heuristics and hard-coded exploration rules, which limit the autonomy of LLM agents. We present CORAL, the first framework for autonomous multi-agent evolution on open-ended problems. CORAL replaces rigid control with long-running agents that explore, reflect, and collaborate through shared persistent memory, asynchronous multi-agent execution, and heartbeat-based interventions. It also provides practical safeguards, including isolated workspaces, evaluator separation, resource management, and agent session and health management. Evaluated on diverse mathematical, algorithmic, and systems optimization tasks, CORAL sets new state-of-the-art results on 10 tasks, achieving 3-10 times higher improvement rates with far fewer evaluations than fixed evolutionary search baselines across tasks. On Anthropic's kernel engineering task, four co-evolving agents improve the best known score from 1363 to 1103 cycles. Mechanistic analyses further show how these gains arise from knowledge reuse and multi-agent exploration and communication. Together, these results suggest that greater agent autonomy and multi-agent evolution can substantially improve open-ended discovery. Code is available atthis https URL.

2604.02296.abstract: Existing video object removal methods excel at inpainting content "behind" the object and correcting appearance-level artifacts such as shadows and reflections. However, when the removed object has more significant interactions, such as collisions with other objects, current models fail to correct them and produce implausible results. We present VOID, a video object removal framework designed to perform physically-plausible inpainting in these complex scenarios. To train the model, we generate a new paired dataset of counterfactual object removals using Kubric and HUMOTO, where removing an object requires altering downstream physical interactions. During inference, a vision-language model identifies regions of the scene affected by the removed object. These regions are then used to guide a video diffusion model that generates physically consistent counterfactual outcomes. Experiments on both synthetic and real data show that our approach better preserves consistent scene dynamics after object removal compared to prior video object removal methods. We hope this framework sheds light on how to make video editing models better simulators of the world through high-level causal reasoning.

2604.01001.abstract: We introduce EgoSim, a closed-loop egocentric world simulator that generates spatially consistent interaction videos and persistently updates the underlying 3D scene state for continuous simulation. Existing egocentric simulators either lack explicit 3D grounding, causing structural drift under viewpoint changes, or treat the scene as static, failing to update world states across multi-stage interactions. EgoSim addresses both limitations by modeling 3D scenes as updatable world states. We generate embodiment interactions via a Geometry-action-aware Observation Simulation model, with spatial consistency from an Interaction-aware State Updating module. To overcome the critical data bottleneck posed by the difficulty in acquiring densely aligned scene-interaction training pairs, we design a scalable pipeline that extracts static point clouds, camera trajectories, and embodiment actions from in-the-wild large-scale monocular egocentric videos. We further introduce EgoCap, a capture system that enables low-cost real-world data collection with uncalibrated smartphones. Extensive experiments demonstrate that EgoSim significantly outperforms existing methods in terms of visual quality, spatial consistency, and generalization to complex scenes and in-the-wild dexterous interactions, while supporting cross-embodiment transfer to robotic manipulation. Codes and datasets will be open soon. The project page is atthis http URL.
2604.01001.comments: Project Page: this http URL

2604.01202.abstract: We consider the question: when a large language reasoning model makes a choice, did it think first and then decide to, or decide first and then think? In this paper, we present evidence that detectable, early-encoded decisions shape chain-of-thought in reasoning models. Specifically, we show that a simple linear probe successfully decodes tool-calling decisions from pre-generation activations with very high confidence, and in some cases, even before a single reasoning token is produced. Activation steering supports this causally: perturbing the decision direction leads to inflated deliberation, and flips behavior in many examples (between 7 - 79% depending on model and benchmark). We also show through behavioral analysis that, when steering changes the decision, the chain-of-thought process often rationalizes the flip rather than resisting it. Together, these results suggest that reasoning models can encode action choices before they begin to deliberate in text.

2604.02097.abstract: Unified models (UMs) hold promise for their ability to understand and generate content across heterogeneous modalities. Compared to merely generating visual content, the use of UMs for interleaved cross-modal reasoning is more promising and valuable, e.g., for solving understanding problems that require dense visual thinking, improving visual generation through self-reflection, or modeling visual dynamics of the physical world guided by stepwise action interventions. However, existing UMs necessitate pixel decoding as a bridge due to their disjoint visual representations for understanding and generation, which is both ineffective and inefficient. In this paper, we introduce LatentUM, a novel unified model that represents all modalities within a shared semantic latent space, eliminating the need for pixel-space mediation between visual understanding and generation. This design naturally enables flexible interleaved cross-modal reasoning and generation. Beyond improved computational efficiency, the shared representation substantially alleviates codec bias and strengthens cross-modal alignment, allowing LatentUM to achieve state-of-the-art performance on the Visual Spatial Planning benchmark, push the limits of visual generation through self-reflection, and support world modeling by predicting future visual states within the shared semantic latent space.

2604.01973.abstract: When evaluating identity-focused tasks such as personalized generation and image editing, existing vision encoders entangle object identity with background context, leading to unreliable representations and metrics. We introduce the first principled framework to address this vulnerability using Near-identity (NearID) distractors, where semantically similar but distinct instances are placed on the exact same background as a reference image, eliminating contextual shortcuts and isolating identity as the sole discriminative signal. Based on this principle, we present the NearID dataset (19K identities, 316K matched-context distractors) together with a strict margin-based evaluation protocol. Under this setting, pre-trained encoders perform poorly, achieving Sample Success Rates (SSR), a strict margin-based identity discrimination metric, as low as 30.7% and often ranking distractors above true cross-view matches. We address this by learning identity-aware representations on a frozen backbone using a two-tier contrastive objective enforcing the hierarchy: same identity > NearID distractor > random negative. This improves SSR to 99.2%, enhances part-level discrimination by 28.0%, and yields stronger alignment with human judgments on DreamBench++, a human-aligned benchmark for personalization. Project page:this https URL
2604.01973.comments: Code at this https URL

2604.01007.abstract: AI agents increasingly operate over extended time horizons, yet their ability to retain, organize, and recall multimodal experiences remains a critical bottleneck. Building effective lifelong memory requires navigating a vast design space spanning architecture, retrieval strategies, prompt engineering, and data pipelines; this space is too large and interconnected for manual exploration or traditional AutoML to explore effectively. We deploy an autonomous research pipeline to discover Omni-SimpleMem, a unified multimodal memory framework for lifelong AI agents. Starting from a naïve baseline (F1=0.117 on LoCoMo), the pipeline autonomously executes ${\sim}50$ experiments across two benchmarks, diagnosing failure modes, proposing architectural modifications, and repairing data pipeline bugs, all without human intervention in the inner loop. The resulting system achieves state-of-the-art on both benchmarks, improving F1 by +411% on LoCoMo (0.117$\to$0.598) and +214% on Mem-Gallery (0.254$\to$0.797) relative to the initial configurations. Critically, the most impactful discoveries are not hyperparameter adjustments: bug fixes (+175%), architectural changes (+44%), and prompt engineering (+188% on specific categories) each individually exceed the cumulative contribution of all hyperparameter tuning, demonstrating capabilities fundamentally beyond the reach of traditional AutoML. We provide a taxonomy of six discovery types and identify four properties that make multimodal memory particularly suited for autoresearch, offering guidance for applying autonomous research pipelines to other AI system domains. Code is available at thisthis https URL.

2603.29640.abstract: Can AI accelerate the development of AI itself? While recent agentic systems have shown strong performance on well-scoped tasks with rapid feedback, it remains unclear whether they can tackle the costly, long-horizon, and weakly supervised research loops that drive real AI progress. We present ASI-Evolve, an agentic framework for AI-for-AI research that closes this loop through a learn-design-experiment-analyze cycle. ASI-Evolve augments standard evolutionary agents with two key components: a cognition base that injects accumulated human priors into each round of exploration, and a dedicated analyzer that distills complex experimental outcomes into reusable insights for future iterations. To our knowledge, ASI-Evolve is the first unified framework to demonstrate AI-driven discovery across three central components of AI development: data, architectures, and learning algorithms. In neural architecture design, it discovered 105 SOTA linear attention architectures, with the best discovered model surpassing DeltaNet by +0.97 points, nearly 3x the gain of recent human-designed improvements. In pretraining data curation, the evolved pipeline improves average benchmark performance by +3.96 points, with gains exceeding 18 points on MMLU. In reinforcement learning algorithm design, discovered algorithms outperform GRPO by up to +12.5 points on AMC32, +11.67 points on AIME24, and +5.04 points on OlympiadBench. We further provide initial evidence that this AI-for-AI paradigm can transfer beyond the AI stack through experiments in mathematics and biomedicine. Together, these results suggest that ASI-Evolve represents a promising step toward enabling AI to accelerate AI across the foundational stages of development, offering early evidence for the feasibility of closed-loop AI research.
2603.29640.comments: 19 pages, 6 figures, 6 tables. Code available at this https URL

2604.02190.abstract: Vision-Language-Action (VLA) models have recently emerged in autonomous driving, with the promise of leveraging rich world knowledge to improve the cognitive capabilities of driving systems. However, adapting such models for driving tasks currently faces a critical dilemma between spatial perception and semantic reasoning. Consequently, existing VLA systems are forced into suboptimal compromises: directly adopting 2D Vision-Language Models yields limited spatial perception, whereas enhancing them with 3D spatial representations often impairs the native reasoning capacity of VLMs. We argue that this dilemma largely stems from the coupled optimization of spatial perception and semantic reasoning within shared model parameters. To overcome this, we propose UniDriveVLA, a Unified Driving Vision-Language-Action model based on Mixture-of-Transformers that addresses the perception-reasoning conflict via expert decoupling. Specifically, it comprises three experts for driving understanding, scene perception, and action planning, which are coordinated through masked joint attention. In addition, we combine a sparse perception paradigm with a three-stage progressive training strategy to improve spatial perception while maintaining semantic reasoning capability. Extensive experiments show that UniDriveVLA achieves state-of-the-art performance in open-loop evaluation on nuScenes and closed-loop evaluation on Bench2Drive. Moreover, it demonstrates strong performance across a broad range of perception, prediction, and understanding tasks, including 3D detection, online mapping, motion forecasting, and driving-oriented VQA, highlighting its broad applicability as a unified model for autonomous driving. Code and model have been released atthis https URL
2604.02190.comments: code has been released at this https URL

2604.00917.abstract: The rise of large language models for code has reshaped software development. Autonomous coding agents, able to create branches, open pull requests, and perform code reviews, now actively contribute to real-world projects. Their growing role offers a unique and timely opportunity to investigate AI-driven contributions and their effects on code quality, team dynamics, and software maintainability. In this work, we construct a novel dataset of approximately $110,000$ open-source pull requests, including associated commits, comments, reviews, issues, and file changes, collectively representing millions of lines of source code. We compare five popular coding agents, including OpenAI Codex, Claude Code, GitHub Copilot, Google Jules, and Devin, examining how their usage differs in various development aspects such as merge frequency, edited file types, and developer interaction signals, including comments and reviews. Furthermore, we emphasize that code authoring and review are only a small part of the larger software engineering process, as the resulting code must also be maintained and updated over time. Hence, we offer several longitudinal estimates of survival and churn rates for agent-generated versus human-authored code. Ultimately, our findings indicate an increasing agent activity in open-source projects, although their contributions are associated with more churn over time compared to human-authored code.

2603.27666.abstract: Recent advances in diffusion-based controllable visual generation have led to remarkable improvements in image quality. However, these powerful models are typically deployed on cloud servers due to their large computational demands, raising serious concerns about user data privacy. To enable secure and efficient on-device generation, we explore in this paper controllable diffusion models built upon linear attention architectures, which offer superior scalability and efficiency, even on edge devices. Yet, our experiments reveal that existing controllable generation frameworks, such as ControlNet and OminiControl, either lack the flexibility to support multiple heterogeneous condition types or suffer from slow convergence on such linear-attention models. To address these limitations, we propose a novel controllable diffusion framework tailored for linear attention backbones like SANA. The core of our method lies in a unified gated conditioning module working in a dual-path pipeline, which effectively integrates multi-type conditional inputs, such as spatially aligned and non-aligned cues. Extensive experiments on multiple tasks and benchmarks demonstrate that our approach achieves state-of-the-art controllable generation performance based on linear-attention models, surpassing existing methods in terms of fidelity and controllability.

2604.01569.abstract: Recent video multimodal large language models achieve impressive results across various benchmarks. However, current evaluations suffer from two critical limitations: (1) inflated scores can mask deficiencies in fine-grained visual understanding and reasoning, and (2) answer correctness is often measured without verifying whether models identify the precise spatio-temporal evidence supporting their predictions. To address this, we present VideoZeroBench, a hierarchical benchmark designed for challenging long-video question answering that rigorously verifies spatio-temporal evidence. It comprises 500 manually annotated questions across 13 domains, paired with temporal intervals and spatial bounding boxes as evidence. To disentangle answering generation, temporal grounding, and spatial grounding, we introduce a five-level evaluation protocol that progressively tightens evidence requirements. Experiments show that even Gemini-3-Pro correctly answers fewer than 17% of questions under the standard end-to-end QA setting (Level-3). When grounding constraints are imposed, performance drops sharply: No model exceeds 1% accuracy when both correct answering and accurate spatio-temporal localization are required (Level-5), with most failing to achieve any correct grounded predictions. These results expose a significant gap between surface-level answer correctness and genuine evidence-based reasoning, revealing that grounded video understanding remains a bottleneck for long-video QA. We further analyze performance across minimal evidence spans, atomic abilities, and inference paradigms, providing insights for future research in grounded video reasoning. The benchmark and code will be made publicly available.

2604.01676.abstract: GUI Process Automation (GPA) is a lightweight but general vision-based Robotic Process Automation (RPA), which enables fast and stable process replay with only a single demo. Addressing the fragility of traditional RPA and the non-deterministic risks of current vision language model-based GUI agents, GPA introduces three core benefits: (1) Robustness via Sequential Monte Carlo-based localization to handle rescaling and detection uncertainty; (2) Deterministic and Reliability safeguarded by readiness calibration; and (3) Privacy through fast, fully local execution. This approach delivers the adaptability, robustness, and security required for enterprise workflows. It can also be used as an MCP/CLI tool by other agents with coding capabilities so that the agent only reasons and orchestrates while GPA handles the GUI execution. We conducted a pilot experiment to compare GPA with Gemini 3 Pro (with CUA tools) and found that GPA achieves higher success rate with 10 times faster execution speed in finishing long-horizon GUI tasks.

2604.01618.abstract: Vision-language-action (VLA) models have shown strong performance in robotic manipulation, yet their robustness to physically realizable adversarial attacks remains underexplored. Existing studies reveal vulnerabilities through language perturbations and 2D visual attacks, but these attack surfaces are either less representative of real deployment or limited in physical realism. In contrast, adversarial 3D textures pose a more physically plausible and damaging threat, as they are naturally attached to manipulated objects and are easier to deploy in physical environments. Bringing adversarial 3D textures to VLA systems is nevertheless nontrivial. A central obstacle is that standard 3D simulators do not provide a differentiable optimization path from the VLA objective function back to object appearance, making it difficult to optimize through an end-to-end manner. To address this, we introduce Foreground-Background Decoupling (FBD), which enables differentiable texture optimization through dual-renderer alignment while preserving the original simulation environment. To further ensure that the attack remains effective across long-horizon and diverse viewpoints in the physical world, we propose Trajectory-Aware Adversarial Optimization (TAAO), which prioritizes behaviorally critical frames and stabilizes optimization with a vertex-based parameterization. Built on these designs, we present Tex3D, the first framework for end-to-end optimization of 3D adversarial textures directly within the VLA simulation environment. Experiments in both simulation and real-robot settings show that Tex3D significantly degrades VLA performance across multiple manipulation tasks, achieving task failure rates of up to 96.7\%. Our empirical results expose critical vulnerabilities of VLA systems to physically grounded 3D adversarial attacks and highlight the need for robustness-aware training.

2603.30043.abstract: Video diffusion models exhibit emergent reasoning capabilities like solving mazes and puzzles, yet little is understood about how they reason during generation. We take a first step towards understanding this and study the internal planning dynamics of video models using 2D maze solving as a controlled testbed. Our investigations reveal two findings. Our first finding is early plan commitment: video diffusion models commit to a high-level motion plan within the first few denoising steps, after which further denoising alters visual details but not the underlying trajectory. Our second finding is that path length, not obstacle density, is the dominant predictor of maze difficulty, with a sharp failure threshold at 12 steps. This means video models can only reason over long mazes by chaining together multiple sequential generations. To demonstrate the practical benefits of our findings, we introduce Chaining with Early Planning, or ChEaP, which only spends compute on seeds with promising early plans and chains them together to tackle complex mazes. This improves accuracy from 7% to 67% on long-horizon mazes and by 2.5x overall on hard tasks in Frozen Lake and VR-Bench across Wan2.2-14B and HunyuanVideo-1.5. Our analysis reveals that current video models possess deeper reasoning capabilities than previously recognized, which can be elicited more reliably with better inference-time scaling.

2603.28068.abstract: Although image generation has boosted various applications via its rapid evolution, whether the state-of-the-art models are able to produce ready-to-use academic illustrations for papers is still largely unexplored. Directly comparing or evaluating the illustration with VLM is native but requires oracle multi-modal understanding ability, which is unreliable for long and complex texts and illustrations. To address this, we propose AIBench, the first benchmark using VQA for evaluating logic correctness of the academic illustrations and VLMs for assessing aesthetics. In detail, we designed four levels of questions proposed from a logic diagram summarized from the method part of the paper, which query whether the generated illustration aligns with the paper on different scales. Our VQA-based approach raises more accurate and detailed evaluations on visual-logical consistency while relying less on the ability of the judger VLM. With our high-quality AIBench, we conduct extensive experiments and conclude that the performance gap between models on this task is significantly larger than general ones, reflecting their various complex reasoning and high-density generation ability. Further, the logic and aesthetics are hard to optimize simultaneously as in handcrafted illustrations. Additional experiments further state that test-time scaling on both abilities significantly boosts the performance on this task.

2604.02289.abstract: Recent multimodal large language models have achieved strong performance in unified text and image understanding and generation, yet extending such native capability to 3D remains challenging due to limited data. Compared to abundant 2D imagery, high-quality 3D assets are scarce, making 3D synthesis under-constrained. Existing methods often rely on indirect pipelines that edit in 2D and lift results into 3D via optimization, sacrificing geometric consistency. We present Omni123, a 3D-native foundation model that unifies text-to-2D and text-to-3D generation within a single autoregressive framework. Our key insight is that cross-modal consistency between images and 3D can serve as an implicit structural constraint. By representing text, images, and 3D as discrete tokens in a shared sequence space, the model leverages abundant 2D data as a geometric prior to improve 3D representations. We introduce an interleaved X-to-X training paradigm that coordinates diverse cross-modal tasks over heterogeneous paired datasets without requiring fully aligned text-image-3D triplets. By traversing semantic-visual-geometric cycles (e.g., text to image to 3D to image) within autoregressive sequences, the model jointly enforces semantic alignment, appearance fidelity, and multi-view geometric consistency. Experiments show that Omni123 significantly improves text-guided 3D generation and editing, demonstrating a scalable path toward multimodal 3D world models.

2604.01666.abstract: Despite recent progress, video diffusion models still struggle to synthesize realistic videos involving highly dynamic motions or requiring fine-grained motion controllability. A central limitation lies in the scarcity of such examples in commonly used training datasets. To address this, we introduce DynaVid, a video synthesis framework that leverages synthetic motion data in training, which is represented as optical flow and rendered using computer graphics pipelines. This approach offers two key advantages. First, synthetic motion offers diverse motion patterns and precise control signals that are difficult to obtain from real data. Second, unlike rendered videos with artificial appearances, rendered optical flow encodes only motion and is decoupled from appearance, thereby preventing models from reproducing the unnatural look of synthetic videos. Building on this idea, DynaVid adopts a two-stage generation framework: a motion generator first synthesizes motion, and then a motion-guided video generator produces video frames conditioned on that motion. This decoupled formulation enables the model to learn dynamic motion patterns from synthetic data while preserving visual realism from real-world videos. We validate our framework on two challenging scenarios, vigorous human motion generation and extreme camera motion control, where existing datasets are particularly limited. Extensive experiments demonstrate that DynaVid improves the realism and controllability in dynamic motion generation and camera motion control.
2604.01666.comments: Accepted to CVPR 2026. Website: this https URL

2604.01014.abstract: Membership Inference Attacks (MIAs) serve as a fundamental auditing tool for evaluating training data leakage in machine learning models. However, existing methodologies predominantly rely on static, handcrafted heuristics that lack adaptability, often leading to suboptimal performance when transferred across different large models. In this work, we propose AutoMIA, an agentic framework that reformulates membership inference as an automated process of self-exploration and strategy evolution. Given high-level scenario specifications, AutoMIA self-explores the attack space by generating executable logits-level strategies and progressively refining them through closed-loop evaluation feedback. By decoupling abstract strategy reasoning from low-level execution, our framework enables a systematic, model-agnostic traversal of the attack search space. Extensive experiments demonstrate that AutoMIA consistently matches or outperforms state-of-the-art baselines while eliminating the need for manual feature engineering.

2604.02007.abstract: Building general-purpose reasoning models using reinforcement learning with verifiable rewards (RLVR) across diverse domains has been widely adopted by frontier open-weight models. However, their training recipes and domain mixtures are often not disclosed. Joint optimization across domains poses significant challenges: domains vary widely in rollout length, problem difficulty and sample efficiency. Further, models with long chain-of-thought traces increase inference cost and latency, making efficiency critical for practical deployment. We present Apriel-Reasoner, trained with a fully reproducible multi-domain RL post-training recipe on Apriel-Base, a 15B-parameter open-weight LLM, across five domains using public datasets: mathematics, code generation, instruction following, logical puzzles and function calling. We introduce an adaptive domain sampling mechanism that preserves target domain ratios despite heterogeneous rollout dynamics, and a difficulty-aware extension of the standard length penalty that, with no additional training overhead, encourages longer reasoning for difficult problems and shorter traces for easy ones. Trained with a strict 16K-token output budget, Apriel-Reasoner generalizes to 32K tokens at inference and improves over Apriel-Base on AIME 2025, GPQA, MMLU-Pro, and LiveCodeBench while producing 30-50% shorter reasoning traces. It matches strong open-weight models of similar size at lower token cost, thereby pushing the Pareto frontier of accuracy versus token budget.

2603.28130.abstract: We introduce Multilingual Document Parsing Benchmark, the first benchmark for multilingual digital and photographed document parsing. Document parsing has made remarkable strides, yet almost exclusively on clean, digital, well-formatted pages in a handful of dominant languages. No systematic benchmark exists to evaluate how models perform on digital and photographed documents across diverse scripts and low-resource languages. MDPBench comprises 3,400 document images spanning 17 languages, diverse scripts, and varied photographic conditions, with high-quality annotations produced through a rigorous pipeline of expert model labeling, manual correction, and human verification. To ensure fair comparison and prevent data leakage, we maintain separate public and private evaluation splits. Our comprehensive evaluation of both open-source and closed-source models uncovers a striking finding: while closed-source models (notably Gemini3-Pro) prove relatively robust, open-source alternatives suffer dramatic performance collapse, particularly on non-Latin scripts and real-world photographed documents, with an average drop of 17.8% on photographed documents and 14.0% on non-Latin scripts. These results reveal significant performance imbalances across languages and conditions, and point to concrete directions for building more inclusive, deployment-ready parsing systems. Source available atthis https URL.

2604.01760.abstract: Autoregressive neural codec language models have shown strong zero-shot voice cloning ability, but decoder-only architectures treat input text as a prefix that competes with the growing audio sequence for positional capacity, weakening text conditioning over long utterances. We present T5Gemma-TTS, an encoder-decoder codec language model that maintains persistent text conditioning by routing bidirectional text representations through cross-attention at every decoder layer. Built on the T5Gemma pretrained encoder-decoder backbone (2B encoder + 2B decoder; 4B parameters), it inherits rich linguistic knowledge without phoneme conversion and processes text directly at the subword level. To improve duration control, we introduce Progress-Monitoring Rotary Position Embedding (PM-RoPE) in all 26 cross-attention layers, injecting normalized progress signals that help the decoder track target speech length. Trained on 170,000 hours of multilingual speech in English, Chinese, and Japanese, T5Gemma-TTS achieves a statistically significant speaker-similarity gain on Japanese over XTTSv2 (0.677 vs. 0.622; non-overlapping 95% confidence intervals) and the highest numerical Korean speaker similarity (0.747) despite Korean not being included in training, although this margin over XTTSv2 (0.741) is not statistically conclusive. It also attains the lowest numerical Japanese character error rate among five baselines (0.126), though this ranking should be interpreted cautiously because of partial confidence-interval overlap with Kokoro. English results on LibriSpeech should be viewed as an upper-bound estimate because LibriHeavy is a superset of LibriSpeech. Using the same checkpoint, disabling PM-RoPE at inference causes near-complete synthesis failure: CER degrades from 0.129 to 0.982 and duration accuracy drops from 79% to 46%. Code and weights are available atthis https URL.

2604.01328.abstract: Traditional scientific discovery relies on an iterative hypothesise-experiment-refine cycle that has driven progress for centuries, but its intuitive, ad-hoc implementation often wastes resources, yields inefficient designs, and misses critical insights. This tutorial presents Bayesian Optimisation (BO), a principled probability-driven framework that formalises and automates this core scientific cycle. BO uses surrogate models (e.g., Gaussian processes) to model empirical observations as evolving hypotheses, and acquisition functions to guide experiment selection, balancing exploitation of known knowledge and exploration of uncharted domains to eliminate guesswork and manual trial-and-error. We first frame scientific discovery as an optimisation problem, then unpack BO's core components, end-to-end workflows, and real-world efficacy via case studies in catalysis, materials science, organic synthesis, and molecule discovery. We also cover critical technical extensions for scientific applications, including batched experimentation, heteroscedasticity, contextual optimisation, and human-in-the-loop integration. Tailored for a broad audience, this tutorial bridges AI advances in BO with practical natural science applications, offering tiered content to empower cross-disciplinary researchers to design more efficient experiments and accelerate principled scientific discovery.

2604.01298.abstract: Anticipating supply chain disruptions before they materialize is a core challenge for firms and policymakers alike. A key difficulty is learning to reason reliably about infrequent, high-impact events from noisy and unstructured inputs - a setting where general-purpose models struggle without task-specific adaptation. We introduce an end-to-end framework that trains LLMs to produce calibrated probabilistic forecasts using realized disruption outcomes as supervision. The resulting model substantially outperforms strong baselines - including GPT-5 - on accuracy, calibration, and precision. We also show that training induces more structured and reliable probabilistic reasoning without explicit prompting. These results suggest a general pathway for training domain-specific forecasting models that produce decision-ready signals. To support transparency we open-source the evaluation dataset used in this study.Dataset:this https URL

2604.02330.abstract: Recent advances in video diffusion have enabled the development of "world models" capable of simulating interactive environments. However, these models are largely restricted to single-agent settings, failing to control multiple agents simultaneously in a scene. In this work, we tackle a fundamental issue of action binding in existing video diffusion models, which struggle to associate specific actions with their corresponding subjects. For this purpose, we propose ActionParty, an action controllable multi-subject world model for generative video games. It introduces subject state tokens, i.e. latent variables that persistently capture the state of each subject in the scene. By jointly modeling state tokens and video latents with a spatial biasing mechanism, we disentangle global video frame rendering from individual action-controlled subject updates. We evaluate ActionParty on the Melting Pot benchmark, demonstrating the first video world model capable of controlling up to seven players simultaneously across 46 diverse environments. Our results show significant improvements in action-following accuracy and identity consistency, while enabling robust autoregressive tracking of subjects through complex interactions.
